title: Di & Ian, Windmill Hill, Avebury, Devizes, Lacock, Bradford & Bath

main_route.excerpt: Then on to Di + Ian at, [?Nortom] [?Combe], [Waterbourne written above in different ink/annotation]. We stayed the night in their garden, using the bathroom in the bungalow.
We went for a walk up Windmill Hill.
The next morning we saw the Great Barn museum at Avebury, + walked around the Standing Stones.

main_route.normalized_name: West Kennet Long Barrow, Wiltshire, UK
main_route.excerpt: In the afternoon we walked up to West Kennet Long Barrow, into the Neolithic Burial Chamber.
We drove on and looked around Devizes. Antique Book Shop, drinks.
We camped at the Lakeside, Rowde ***. Lots of water, glad to have left John + Charles behind.
We went to Bowden Hill, St. Anne, [?lay reader] for Church BCP communion.
Then on to Lacock, museum for Fox-Talbot, inventor of photography 1839.
Bradford also has beautiful stonehouses, 2 churches, one a lovely Saxon chapel. A delicious cream tea at the Bridge Tea Room.

main_route.normalized_name: Stowford Farm, near Trowbridge, Wiltshire, UK
main_route.excerpt: On towards Bath, we are spending the night at Stowford Farm, west of Trowbridge. Originally a mill, again lots of water, very, very pretty.
Bath Peter map read. We've brought Thomas a plaster angel with guitar. We visited the Roman Baths with

unresolved.issue: Location of Di & Ian's home is unclear; diary text is partially illegible. 'Nortom Combe' may be Norton or Nortom; 'Waterbourne' written in different ink as annotation, possibly a village name or house name. Location in Wiltshire area likely but unconfirmed.
unresolved.excerpt: Then on to Di + Ian at, [?Nortom] [?Combe], [Waterbourne written above in different ink/annotation].

boundary_flags: A_0013_R is boundary_mixed=true; Bath diary entry ends mid-sentence and the next trip begins on this page.
summary: A spring weekend camping trip from Birmingham to Wiltshire and Somerset, visiting friends Di and Ian, then touring Windmill Hill, Avebury standing stones, West Kennet Long Barrow, Devizes, Lacock (Fox-Talbot museum), and Bradford-on-Avon, with overnight camps at Rowde and Stowford Farm near Trowbridge, ending with a visit to Bath's Roman Baths. John and Charles were left with grandparents; travellers were Simon, Eve, Thomas, William, and Peter.
extraction_notes: The diary pages carry ISO dates of 1989 but the trip_id is 1990 and date_guess is 1990-04-20; the year in the page dates is likely a transcription artefact and 1990 has been used throughout. The trip covers April 20th and 21st explicitly but the Bath visit is undated (A_0013_R). The page ends mid-sentence ('We visited the Roman Baths with') indicating the diary continues on a page not in this bundle; return to Birmingham is inferred. Di & Ian's exact location is the main unresolved item. Stowford Farm (seq 11) may be identifiable as a campsite near Wingfield or Farleigh Hungerford west of Trowbridge.
